title: Ensure address space layout randomization is enabled

prose.en: Ensure address space layout randomization is enabled

description.en: Address space layout randomization (ASLR) is an exploit mitigation technique which randomly arranges the address space of key data areas of a process.

rationale.en: Randomly placing virtual memory regions will make it difficult to write memory page exploits as the memory placement will be consistently shifting.

audit.en:
Set the following parameter in `/etc/sysctl.conf` or a file in `/etc/sysctl.d/` ending in `.conf`:  

- `kernel.randomize_va_space = 2`

*Example:*  

Run the following command to set the active kernel parameter:  

**Note:** If these settings appear in a canonically later file, or later in the same file, these settings will be overwritten

```
# sysctl -w kernel.randomize_va_space=2
```

```
# printf "%s\n" "kernel.randomize_va_space = 2" >> /etc/sysctl.d/60-kernel_sysctl.conf
```

remediation.en:
Set the following parameter in `/etc/sysctl.conf` or a file in `/etc/sysctl.d/` ending in `.conf`:  

- `kernel.randomize_va_space = 2`

*Example:*  

Run the following command to set the active kernel parameter:  

**Note:** If these settings appear in a canonically later file, or later in the same file, these settings will be overwritten

```
# sysctl -w kernel.randomize_va_space=2
```

```
# printf "%s\n" "kernel.randomize_va_space = 2" >> /etc/sysctl.d/60-kernel_sysctl.conf
```

references: CCI-000366: The organization implements the security configuration settings
